title: Cu fundu-n două luntri
summary: PSD mayors from Iași county boycotted a meeting with Prime Minister Bolojan, claiming it would legitimize austerity, despite PSD having the most ministers in government. The article analyzes PSD's increasing duplicity and hypocrisy, drawing parallels with Slovakia's SMER party which was expelled from the European socialist family (PES) after allying with nationalists.
talking_points: PSD mayors boycotted a meeting with PM Bolojan while PSD simultaneously holds the most ministerial positions in government
Opposition figures like Valeriu Stoica and Băsescu inadvertently encourage PSD's hypocritical behavior by warning about PSD's potential 'political death' or the dangers of a PSD-AUR alliance
The Slovak SMER party provides a cautionary parallel - expelled from PES after governing with nationalists, choosing domestic power over European socialist credentials
PSD, now polling at 22%, behaves duplicitously by trying to simultaneously be in government and in opposition
PSD cares about its European socialist family reputation far less than it cares about holding power domestically
author: Cristian Preda
